title: Foldable Urination Device
abstract: A foldable urination device comprises a body having left and right curved fold lines and left and right side fold lines. Left and right long tabs are connected to respective left and right sides of the body. The left curved fold line and the left side fold lines are disposed on the left side of the body, and the right curved fold line and the right side fold line are disposed on a right side of the body, wherein the central fold line is disposed between the left and right curved fold lines and side fold lines. When the foldable urination device is folded along the curved and side fold lines, the foldable urination device forms a curved passageway having an entrance and an exit. The curved fold line has greater curvature on a top part of the body and lesser curvature on a bottom part of the body. The left long tab is adhered to the right side of the body. A folded handle is attached to the left long tab.
claims:
Claims Claims What is claimed is: 1. A foldable urination device, comprising: a body having a central fold line, left and right curved fold lines and a left side fold line; a left long tab connected to a left side of the body along the left side fold line; wherein the left curved fold line and the left side fold lines are disposed on the left side of the body, and the right curved fold line, wherein the central fold line is disposed between the left and right curved fold lines; wherein when the foldable urination device is folded along the curved and side fold lines, the foldable urination device forms a curved passageway having an entrance and an exit, wherein a user of the urination device is able to hold the urination device directing a urine stream away from the user. 2. The urination device of claim 1, further comprising a right fold line disposed on a right side of the body and a right long tab connected to the right side of the body along the right fold line. 3. The urination device of claim 2, wherein the body is adapted to be folded in at least half of its original size using one or more folding lines as guides. 4. The urination device of claim 2, wherein the urination device comprises a flat sheet and the fold lines are scored lines. 5. The urination device of claim 2, further comprising top, bottom, left and right perimeter edges, wherein the top perimeter edge is an arcuate edge. 6. The urination device of claim 2, further comprising top, bottom, left and right perimeter edges, wherein the bottom perimeter edge comprises short tabs attached to the bottom perimeter edge. 7. The urination device of claim 2, wherein in a folded state, the device comprises four edges having at least one curved edge. 8. The urination device of claim 2, wherein in a folded state, the entrance is larger than the exit. 9. The urination device of claim 1, further comprising a minor tab connected to the left long tab. 10. The urination device of claim 9, when the urination device is in a folded state, the left long tab is adhered to the right side of the body. 11. The urination device of claim 2, further comprising a handle attached to one of the long tabs and the long tabs being folded along a side fold line. 12. The urination device of claim 2, wherein the left and right long tabs are attached to each other by their surfaces. 13. The urination device of claim 6, wherein a surface of a short tab is adhered to the body. 14. The urination device of claim 2, wherein the left and right curved fold lines and the left and right side fold lines are symmetrically disposed on either side of the central fold line. 15. A foldable urination device, comprising: a body having left and right curved fold lines and left and right side fold lines; left and right long tabs connected to respective left and right sides of the body; wherein the left curved fold line and the left side fold lines are disposed on the left side of the body, and the right curved fold line and the right side fold line are disposed on a right side of the body; 

 wherein when the foldable urination device is folded along the curved and side fold lines, the foldable urination device forms a curved passageway having an entrance and an exit, wherein a user of the urination device is able to hold the urination device directing a urine stream away from the user. 16. The foldable urination device of claim 15, wherein the device is folded and the left and right tabs are adhered to each other. 17. The foldable urination device of claim 15, further comprising a central fold line disposed down a middle of the body. 18. The foldable urination device of claim 15, further comprising a handle attached to one of the tabs and wherein the device is folded into two flat layers resting against each other. 19. A foldable urination device, comprising: a body having left and right curved fold lines and left and right side fold lines; left and right long tabs connected to respective left and right sides of the body; wherein the left curved fold line and the left side fold lines are disposed on the left side of the body, and the right curved fold line and the right side fold line are disposed on a right side of the body, wherein the central fold line is disposed between the left and right curved fold lines and side fold lines; wherein when the foldable urination device is folded along the curved and side fold lines, the foldable urination device forms a curved passageway having an entrance and an exit; wherein the curved fold line has greater curvature on a top part of the body and lesser curvature on a bottom part of the body; wherein the left long tab is adhered to the right side of the body; and wherein a user of the urination device is able to hold the urination device directing a urine stream away from the user. 20. The foldable urination device of claim 19, further comprising a folded handle attached to the left long tab. 21. A urination device, comprising: a body being shaped in a rounded funnel shape when the body is unfolded and expanded in use, the body comprising: a first opening; one or more flaps forming into the first opening; and a second opening smaller than the first opening; and one or more handles attached to a portion of the body close to the first opening so that a user of the urination device is able to hold the urination device via the one or more handles for directing a urine liquid away from the user while the user is using the urination device. 22. The urination device of Claim 21, wherein the one or more handles are adapted to be folded together to be held by the user and unfolded to be stored 23. The urination device of Claim 21, wherein the body is adapted to be folded in at least half of its original size using one or more folding lines as guides. 24. The urination device of Claim 21, wherein the body is adapted to be folded so as to be in a flat storage position. 25. The urination device of Claim 21, wherein the body is adapted to be unfolded and expanded into a rounded funnel shape by using the one or more handles and one or more folding lines as guides. 26. The urination device of Claim 21, wherein the one or more flaps is capable of being folded into a packed storage position and being extended in use in an extended shape. 27. The urination device of Claim 21, wherein the first opening is adapted to receive a urine liquid of the user, and the urine liquid is directed to flow through the body of the urination device and out of the urination device through the second opening to a target location. 28. A urination device, comprising: a body being shaped in a rounded funnel shape when the body is unfolded and expanded in use, the body comprising: a first opening; one or more flaps forming into the first opening; and a second opening smaller than the first opening; and one or more handles being attached to a portion of the body close to the first opening and adapted to direct a urine liquid away from a user while the user is using the urination device, wherein the one or more handles are adapted to be folded together to be held by the user and unfolded to be stored. 29. The urination device of Claim 28, wherein the body is adapted to be folded so as to be in a flat storage position. 30. The urination device of Claim 28, wherein the body is adapted to be unfolded and expanded into a rounded funnel shape by using the one or more handles and one or more folding lines as guides. 31. A urination device, comprising: a body being shaped in a rounded funnel shape when the body is unfolded and expanded in use, the body comprising: 

 a first opening; one or more flaps forming into the first opening; and a second opening smaller than the first opening; and one or more handles attached to a portion of the body close to the first opening, wherein the body is adapted to be unfolded and expanded into a rounded funnel shape by using the one or more handles and one or more folding lines as guides. 32. The urination device of Claim 31, wherein the body is adapted to be folded in at least half of its original size using one or more folding lines as guides. 33. The urination device of Claim 31, wherein the one or more flaps is capable of being folded into a packed storage position and being extended in use in an extended shape. 34. The urination device of Claim 31, wherein the first opening is adapted to receive a urine liquid of the user, and the urine liquid is directed to flow through the body of the urination device and out of the urination device through the second opening to a target location. 35. The urination device of Claim 31, wherein the first opening is adapted to receive a urine liquid of the user, and the urine liquid is directed to flow through the body of the urination device and out of the urination device through the second opening to a target location. 36. A method of using a urination device, comprising: unfolding the urination device from a flat storage position by using one or more folding lines as guides, wherein the urination device comprising: a body being shaped in a rounded funnel shape when the body is unfolded and expanded in use, the body comprising a first opening, one or more flaps forming into the first opening, and a second opening smaller than the first opening; and one or more handles attached to a portion of the body close to the first opening so that a user of the urination device is able to hold the urination device via the one or more handles for directing a urine liquid away from the user while the user is using the urination device; and unfolding the one or more handles. 37. The method of Claim 36, further comprising: unfolding the one or more flaps from a packed storage position into an extended shape. 38. The method of Claim 36, further comprising: holding the urination device by the user using the one or more handles. 39. The method of Claim 36, further comprising: positioning the urination device by the user to receive a urine liquid of the 

 user from the first opening and direct the liquid urine to flow through the body of the urination device and out of the urination device through the second opening to a target location. 40. The method of Claim 36, lurther comprising: holding the urination device by the user using the one or more handles while positioning the urination device by the user to receive a urine liquid of the user from the first opening and direct the liquid urine to flow through the body of the urination device and out of the urination device through the second opening to a target location. 41. A urination device, comprising: a body, comprising: a first opening; a first ring-shaped element positioned close to the first opening; one or more second ring-shaped elements, wherein the first ring-shaped element and the one or more second ring-shaped elements are adapted to be stacked concentrically and stored in a packaged form and extended to an expanded form, and wherein the first ring-shaped element is larger in size than the one or more second ring- shaped elements and is located in the outer most location when stacked concentrically; and a second opening smaller than the first opening; one or more flaps forming into the first opening; and one or more handles attached to the first ring-shaped element so that a user of the urination device is able to hold the urination device via the one or more handles for directing a urine liquid away from the user while the user is using the urination device. 42. The urination device of Claim 41, further comprising a rim connected to the body and the one or more flaps. 43. The urination device of Claim 41, wherein the one or more handles are adapted to be folded together to be held by the user and unfolded to be stored. 44. The urination device of Claim 41, wherein the body is adapted to be folded in at least half of its original size using one or more folding lines as guides. 45. The urination device of Claim 41, wherein the body is adapted to be unfolded and expanded into a rounded funnel shape by using the one or more handles and one or more folding lines as guides. 46. The urination device of Claim 41, wherein the one or more flaps are adapted to be unfolded and expanded to form into the first opening in its expanded shape. 47. The urination device of Claim 41, wherein the one or more flaps are capable of being folded through one or more flap folding lines prior to be stored into the packaged form in its folded shape. 48. The urination device of Claim 41, wherein the first opening is adapted to receive a urine liquid of the user, and the urine liquid is directed to flow through the body of the urination device and out of the urination device through the second opening to a target location. 49. A urination device, comprising: a body, comprising: a first opening; a first ring-shaped element positioned close to the first opening; one or more second ring-shaped elements, wherein the first ring-shaped element and the one or more second ring- shaped elements are adapted to be stacked concentrically and stored in a packaged form and extended to an expanded form, and wherein the first ring-shaped element is larger in size than the one or more second ring- shaped elements and is located in the outer most location when stacked concentrically; and a second opening smaller than the first opening; one or more flaps forming into the first opening; a rim connected to the body and the one or more flaps; and one or more handles being attached to the first ring- shaped element and adapted to direct a urine liquid away from a user while the user is using the urination device. 50. The urination device of Claim 49, wherein the one or more flaps are adapted to be unfolded and expanded to form into the first opening in its expanded shape. 51. The urination device of Claim 49, wherein the one or more flaps are capable of being folded through one or more flap folding lines prior to be stored into the packaged form in its folded shape. 52. The urination device of Claim 49, wherein the first opening is adapted to receive a urine liquid of the user, and the urine liquid is directed to flow through the body of the urination device and out of the urination device through the second opening to a target location. 53. The urination device of Claim 49, wherein the one or more handles are adapted to be folded together to be held by the user and unfolded to be stored. 54. The urination device of Claim 49, wherein the body is adapted to be unfolded and expanded into a rounded funnel shape by using the one or more handles and one or more folding lines as guides. 55. A method of using a urination device, comprising: unfolding the urination device from a flat storage position by using one or more folding lines as guides, wherein the urination device comprising: a body being shaped in a rounded funnel shape when the body is 

 unfolded and expanded in use, the body comprising a first opening, one or more flaps forming into the first opening, and a second opening smaller than the first opening; and one or more handles attached to a portion of the body close to the first opening so that a user of the urination device is able to hold the urination device via the one or more handles for directing a urine liquid away from the user while the user is using the urination device; and unfolding the one or more handles. 56. The method of Claim 55, wherein the urination device further comprises a rim connected to the body and the one or more flaps. 57. The method of Claim 55, further comprising: unfolding the one or more flaps from a packed storage position into an extended shape. 58. The method of Claim 55, further comprising: holding the urination device by the user using the one or more handles. 59. The method of Claim 55, further comprising: positioning the urination device by the user to receive a urine liquid of the user from the first opening and direct the liquid urine to flow through the body of the urination device and out of the urination device through the second opening to a target location. 60. The method of Claim 55, further comprising: holding the urination device by the user using the one or more handles while positioning the urination device by the user to receive a urine liquid of the user from the first opening and direct the liquid urine to flow through the body of the urination device and out of the urination device through the second opening to a target location.
